findings.title: canValidate() delegation fallback grants approve rights across the entire org, bypassing service-scope
findings.description: The final branch of `canValidate()` checks whether the caller is an active delegate of ANY delegator in the system, without scoping the check to the leave's owner, department, or service perimeter. A user delegated by Manager A (who covers Dept A) can therefore approve a pending leave belonging to any user in any other department. This directly contradicts the intention of delegations (which are supposed to cover the delegating manager's own perimeter) and the scope logic enforced everywhere else in the service (`getServiceIds`, `getManagedUserIds`). The `findValidatorForUser()` helper correctly scopes delegation lookups to `delegatorId: managerId` (the leave owner's department manager), but `canValidate()` does not.
findings.root_cause: The delegation lookup at the end of `canValidate()` matches any active delegation where the caller is `delegateId`, with no filter on `delegatorId` nor any perimeter check linking the delegator to the leave's owner.
findings.impact: Any user who has been delegated by even one manager can approve (or reject) leave requests from any other employee in the organization, regardless of service or department perimeter. Affects confidentiality (a delegate sees and can act on leaves outside their scope) and integrity (unauthorized leave approvals).
findings.suggested_fix: After checking the delegator's service perimeter, further restrict the delegation lookup: fetch the leave's `validatorId` (the assigned manager), then query for a delegation scoped to that delegatorId: `{ delegateId: validatorId, delegatorId: leave.validatorId, isActive: true, startDate: { lte: today }, endDate: { gte: today } }`. If `leave.validatorId` is null, fall back to the department manager lookup as in `findValidatorForUser`.
acceptance_criteria: A delegate of Manager A cannot call POST /leaves/:id/approve on a leave whose `validatorId` belongs to Manager B (expect 403).
A delegate of Manager A CAN approve a leave whose `validatorId` is Manager A (expect 200).
Existing unit test for `canValidate` covers cross-department delegation boundary.
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: create two managers M1, M2 each with subordinates, create delegation for M1->D, then have D try to approve a leave belonging to M2's subordinate.
findings.notes: The comment at line 712 acknowledges the correct scope ('COR-005: scope the delegation lookup to the user's own department manager') for findValidatorForUser, but canValidate does not apply the same discipline.

findings.title: ImportLeavesDto.leaves array has no upper-bound limit, enabling memory-exhaustion via large payloads
findings.description: The `leaves` array in `ImportLeavesDto` has no `@ArrayMaxSize()` constraint. While the global body limit is 1 MiB (main.ts), a 1 MiB JSON array of `ImportLeaveDto` objects can contain several thousand rows. The `importLeaves()` service method then iterates all rows, calling `getHolidayKeySet()` (a DB query) on each row inside a transaction, plus `findValidatorForUser()` for each row. This can translate to thousands of sequential DB queries per import call, causing significant CPU/DB load. The `validateImport` endpoint (dry-run) has the same issue and even loads ALL active users into memory upfront. Both endpoints require only `leaves:create` permission (not a restricted admin-only permission), so any contributor-level user can trigger this.
findings.root_cause: No `@ArrayMaxSize(N)` decorator on `ImportLeavesDto.leaves`; no per-request DB query budget inside `importLeaves()`.
findings.impact: Authenticated users with `leaves:create` permission can craft a ~1 MiB JSON payload containing ~5000 leave rows, triggering thousands of DB queries per request. Repeated calls can saturate the database connection pool. The `leaves:create` permission is available to broad roles (CONTRIBUTEUR and above).
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@ArrayMaxSize(500)` (or a configurable limit) to `ImportLeavesDto.leaves`. Consider batching `getHolidayKeySet` calls across the entire date range rather than per row, and caching `findValidatorForUser` results per userId within the batch.
acceptance_criteria: POST /leaves/import with `leaves` containing 501 items returns HTTP 400.
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: POST /leaves/import with payload {"leaves": [... 501 items ...]}, expect 400.
findings.notes: The body limit of 1 MiB (SEC-025) mitigates extreme cases but does not prevent the hundreds-of-rows scenario.

findings.title: CreateLeaveDto.reason and ImportLeaveDto.comment lack @MaxLength, allowing oversized free-text storage
findings.description: The `reason` field in `CreateLeaveDto` (stored as `comment` in the DB) has no `@MaxLength` annotation. The same issue exists for `comment` in `ImportLeaveDto`. ApproveLeaveDto and RejectLeaveDto correctly enforce `@MaxLength(2000)`. Without a consistent ceiling, a caller can submit a very large `reason` string (up to the global 1 MiB body limit), which will be persisted to the `leaves.comment` column. The DB column may have its own length limit (depends on the Prisma schema type), but no application-level guard surfaces a meaningful error before attempting the write.
findings.impact: Large free-text values persist to the DB comment column; if the column is `TEXT` there is no DB-side truncation. Audit trail entries then echo these large strings into the audit payload JSON, inflating audit_logs storage.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@MaxLength(2000)` to `CreateLeaveDto.reason` and `ImportLeaveDto.comment`, consistent with `ApproveLeaveDto.comment` and `RejectLeaveDto.reason`.
acceptance_criteria: POST /leaves with `reason` longer than 2000 chars returns HTTP 400.
POST /leaves/import with a `comment` longer than 2000 chars on any row returns HTTP 400.
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: POST /leaves with reason=<2001-char string>, expect 400.

findings.title: ImportLeaveDto.userEmail uses @IsString/@IsNotEmpty instead of @IsEmail, and CreateLeaveDto.leaveTypeId / ImportLeaveDto lack @IsUUID
findings.description: The `userEmail` field in `ImportLeaveDto` is validated only as a non-empty string, not as a valid email address (`@IsEmail()`). Similarly, `CreateLeaveDto.leaveTypeId` and `UpsertLeaveBalanceDto.leaveTypeId`/`userId` accept any non-empty string rather than a UUID (`@IsUUID()`). Although the service resolves these by DB lookup (preventing actual injection), the absence of format validation means malformed payloads only fail at the DB round-trip (returning a generic not-found) rather than producing an early, clear 400 validation error. This also masks enumeration timing differences.
findings.root_cause: Missing `@IsEmail()` on `ImportLeaveDto.userEmail` and `@IsUUID()` on FK-typed string fields in several leave DTOs.
findings.impact: Malformed email/UUID inputs produce delayed errors (DB lookup) rather than fast early rejection. Minor timing oracle for user enumeration if the lookup timing differs, though Prisma parameterization prevents injection.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@IsEmail()` to `ImportLeaveDto.userEmail`. Add `@IsUUID()` to `CreateLeaveDto.leaveTypeId`, `UpsertLeaveBalanceDto.leaveTypeId`, and `UpsertLeaveBalanceDto.userId` (where non-null). Add `@IsUUID()` to `CreateLeaveDto.targetUserId`.
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: POST /leaves with leaveTypeId='INJECTION_ATTEMPT', expect 400.

findings.title: GET /holidays/import-french: year query param parsed via parseInt without range bounds or ParseIntPipe
findings.description: The `year` query parameter on `POST /holidays/import-french` is received as a raw string and parsed with `parseInt(year, 10)` in the handler body. Unlike the sibling `GET /holidays/year/:year` route (which uses `ParseIntPipe`), this endpoint applies no NestJS pipe validation. A NaN value (e.g. `year=abc`) silently becomes `NaN`, which causes the Easter calculation and Date.UTC calls in `importFrenchHolidays()` to produce invalid dates (`Invalid Date`). No range check is applied either, so `year=1600` or `year=9999` would generate malformed holiday inserts. The endpoint requires `holidays:create` (admin-level) so the attack surface is limited.
findings.root_cause: Manual `parseInt` in the handler instead of `ParseIntPipe` with range validation, inconsistent with the `GET /holidays/year/:year` pattern.
findings.impact: An admin supplying a non-numeric `year` causes silent NaN propagation; `Date.UTC(NaN, ...)` produces `Invalid Date`, likely causing a Prisma write error or inserting a null/invalid date. Low severity given admin-only access.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `@Query('year') year: string` with `@Query('year', new ParseIntPipe({ optional: true })) year?: number` and add a range check in the service (e.g., 2000 ≤ year ≤ 2100) consistent with `ImportSchoolVacationDto`.
acceptance_criteria: POST /holidays/import-french?year=1600 returns HTTP 400 or is rejected by the service.
findings.verification_command: N/A — manual verification: POST /holidays/import-french?year=NaN (as admin), observe response.
findings.notes: The `GET /holidays/year/:year` endpoint correctly uses ParseIntPipe; this inconsistency in the import endpoint is the gap.

verified_clean: All controller routes in leaves.controller.ts, leave-types.controller.ts, holidays.controller.ts, school-vacations.controller.ts use @RequirePermissions or @AllowSelfService — no unguarded mutating routes found
No $queryRawUnsafe or $executeRawUnsafe calls in any production service file (only in *.int.spec.ts test harness)
No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or passwords found in any service or controller
Global ValidationPipe configured with whitelist:true and forbidNonWhitelisted:true in main.ts (prevents mass-assignment)
Global body limit set to 1 MiB (SEC-025) in main.ts Fastify config
Global ThrottlerBehindProxyGuard registered as APP_GUARD in AuthModule; school-vacations import endpoint adds stricter @Throttle (3/min)
ParseUUIDPipe used on all :id path parameters across all four modules
ParseIntPipe used for year param in GET /holidays/year/:year and GET /school-vacations (year query)
RBAC perimeter checks (service scope via getManagedUserIds/getServiceIds) consistently applied in leaves service for findAll, update, remove, cancel, approve, reject, rejectCancellation
No user-supplied URL fields found; school-vacations importFromOpenData uses a hardcoded government URL — no SSRF vector
No file upload endpoints in these four modules — file upload checklist not applicable
JWT authentication enforced globally via JwtAuthGuard APP_GUARD; @AllowSelfService correctly gates authenticated self-service routes
Delegation creation (createDelegation) double-checks delegator permission in service layer even though controller already requires leaves:manage_delegations
leave-types/code/:code passes code to Prisma via parameterized where clause — no SQL injection
leave-types.service.ts: system types protected from delete and sensitive-field updates via isSystem flag check
holidays.service.ts: Easter calculation and all date math use server-side computation only, no user input in date formulas
school-vacations.service.ts importFromOpenData: uses AbortController with 10s timeout on external fetch; URL is not user-controlled; zone/year validated by DTO
CreateLeaveTypeDto: code field validated with @Matches(/^[A-Z_]+$/) and @MaxLength(50); color with @Matches hex pattern — well-constrained
ApproveLeaveDto.comment and RejectLeaveDto.reason both have @MaxLength(2000)
leaves.service.ts findOne: ownership check present — non-owners without manage perimeter get 403
canManageLeave: correctly checks leaves:manage_any before falling back to service perimeter
Serializable transaction + single retry on P2034 used for leave creation (DAT-024) — race condition properly handled
import-leaves.dto.ts: dates validated as @IsString/@IsNotEmpty; service validates via new Date() + isNaN check before inserting
Self-approval (leaves:self_approve) correctly prevented from approving others' leaves (approve() gate: leave.userId === validatorId throws ForbiddenException)
